channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: John Bolton, Ex-Trump Adviser, Reaches Deal to Plead Guilty Over Classified Information
item.description: The former Trump national security adviser plans to plead guilty to a count of illegal retention of classified information, which could result in a fine and possibly prison time of up to five years.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Michael S. Schmidt
item.category: The Room Where It Happened: A White House Memoir (Book)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: John R. Bolton’s indictment accused him of mishandling classified information when he compiled notes for a book that was critical of President Trump.

item.title: Trump’s Arch Project to Face Questions and Criticism at Planning Meeting
item.description: The National Capital Planning Commission received nearly 1,700 comments about President Trump’s plans to build a 250-foot arch in the nation’s capital. Almost all opposed the idea.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump with a model of his proposed arch at the White House last year.

item.title: Trump Says He Plans to Nominate Blanche for Attorney General
item.description: Todd Blanche has been serving as the acting attorney general. A formal nomination to the post would be the president’s latest move to place loyalists in top government jobs.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche during a House Committee on Appropriations, Subcommittee on Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies hearing this week.

item.title: Justice Department Tells Prosecutors to Pursue Immigrant Vote Fraud Cases
item.description: About 90 such investigations are currently open, a Justice Department official told prosecutors. The tally offers a glimpse inside the scale of the administration’s efforts.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Department of Justice in Washington.

item.title: American Farms Rely More Than Ever on the Troubled H-2A Visa Program
item.description: The Trump administration is allowing in more agricultural guest workers under the H-2A program, but preventing abuses is proving difficult.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis
item.media:credit: Grant Hindsley for The New York Times
item.media:description: Christian Calderon is among the H-2A visa workers hired to harvest cherries in Kennewick, Wash. As the Trump administration lowers wages, farmers have requested more foreign workers.

item.title: Early Research Suggests a Path to Predict and Prevent Lung Cancer
item.description: Scientists have identified proteins that could signal increased lung cancer risk, as well as a drug that could reduce the odds of a tumor developing.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal
item.media:credit: Getty Images

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox Riot Shocks Israelis In Latest Protest At Military Draft
item.description: Israel’s Haredim have escalated their protests against military service in recent weeks, underscoring growing divisions ahead of national elections.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner

item.title: Hezbollah’s Fiber-Optic Drones Expose Cracks in Israeli Defenses
item.description: The Lebanese militant group’s attacks have caught Israelis off guard, forcing its political and military leaders to scramble for solutions.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jalaa Marey/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Israeli soldiers reacting to drones near the Israel-Lebanon border on Monday.

item.title: Netflix’s New Movie Strategy: Fewer, Better Films
item.description: Dan Lin doesn’t fawn over stars or write blank checks. And he still greenlights more movies than anyone.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling
item.media:credit: Ahmed Gaber for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dan Lin, the chairman of Netflix’s film division.

item.title: ‘Teachers Are Going to Hate It’: How Social Media Apps Hooked Teens at School
item.description: Internal documents show how tech giants grabbed children’s attention throughout the day, a strategy that schools say has undermined education.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Valentino-DeVries
item.media:credit: Yana Paskova for The New York Times
item.media:description: Social media companies’ push to reach students has often overshadowed concerns from parents and teachers.

item.title: Gwynne Shotwell, Elon Musk’s No. 2 at SpaceX, Is the Company’s Steady Hand
item.description: Gwynne Shotwell, the president and chief operating officer, is the adult-in-the-room foil to Mr. Musk as SpaceX prepares for a blockbuster initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Joe Raedle/Getty Images
item.media:description: Gwynne Shotwell, SpaceX’s president and chief operating officer, in Miami Beach last year during one of her infrequent public appearances. “I love working for Elon,” she has said.

item.title: Who’s Excited for SpaceX’s I.P.O.? Space Nerds.
item.description: Few are as delighted by the pending market debut as the vibrant community of space enthusiasts who want to push discovery of the final frontier.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel
item.media:credit: Mark Wang

item.title: Marjane Satrapi, the Author of ‘Persepolis,’ Dies at 56
item.description: Her popular graphic novel series, published in the early 2000s, followed an Iranian girl through the Islamic Revolution and the Iran-Iraq War.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:credit: Christophe Petit Tesson/EPA, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: Marjane Satrapi in 2023. She was one of the best-known exponents of a form of graphic novel that combined political history and memoir.

item.title: Review: ‘Persepolis,’ by Marjane Satrapi
item.description: A memoir and a history of Iran’s turbulent 20th-century politics, one comic strip frame at a time.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Eberstadt
item.media:credit: Olivia Fields

item.title: Bill Bradley, 2-Time Champion With the Knicks, Cheers on His Old Team in the Finals
item.description: Watching Game 1 of the Knicks-Spurs series, Bill Bradley, the two-time N.B.A. champion and former U.S. senator, spoke reverently of teamwork — and had a few choice words for the refs.
item.dc:creator: Matt Flegenheimer
item.category: A Sense of Where You Are: Bill Bradley at Princeton (Book)
item.media:credit: Associated Press
item.media:description: Bill Bradley won N.B.A. championships with the Knicks in 1970 and 1973.

item.title: Priest’s Ebola Death Stirs Fear and Doubt in Congolese Town
item.description: Priest’s Ebola Death Stirs Fear an Doubt in Congolese Town
item.dc:creator: Arlette Bashizi
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: Why Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass Has to Fight for Her Job
item.description: Ms. Bass, a Democrat, has had a roller-coaster first term. She now faces the first runoff since 2005 for an incumbent mayor in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass, center, campaigning at a community center in Plaza de Valle in Los Angeles, last month.

item.title: The Democrats’ Big Decision: Black Representation or More Blue Seats?
item.description: As Republicans break up majority-Black House districts, Democrats must decide whether to preserve seats concentrated in urban areas or push them into white suburbs to target G.O.P. seats.
item.dc:creator: Clyde McGrady
item.media:credit: Will Crooks for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative James Clyburn addressing the crowd at his annual fish fry alongside Representative Ro Khanna of California, second from left, and Governor Andy Beshear of Kentucky in Columbia, S.C., on Friday.

item.title: The Small-Business Owners Managing Whole Armies of A.I. Employees
item.description: When you turn A.I. agents loose on your finances, email and customers, what could possibly go wrong?
item.dc:creator: Clive Thompson

item.title: Should You Outsource Your Morning Routine to a Chatbot?
item.description: A.I. wants to help you start the day. Its suggestions? Drink coffee and get dressed.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Haigney

item.title: How LinkedIn Found Its Social Platform Era
item.description: Celebrities and paid influencers have transformed the career-focused platform, which for some users, has become a job unto itself.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
item.media:description: “This whole corporate, ‘move the needle,’ synergistic stuff — I think people are tired of it,” said Brooke Sweedar, a content creator on LinkedIn. “You have to do something else.”

item.title: Songwriter David Baerwald Shares an Unspeakable Family Secret in ‘The Fire Agent’
item.description: In “The Fire Agent,” the musician David Baerwald bases his debut novel on the shadowy life of his German-Jewish grandfather: an aristocrat, a soldier, a Buddhist and a spy.
item.dc:creator: Celia McGee and Landon Speers
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: 5 Minutes That Will Make You Love Charlie Parker
item.description: Bird helped usher in bebop and transformed the alto saxophone into an instrument of startling possibility. Here’s an 11-song primer on his enduring legacy.
item.dc:creator: Marcus J. Moore

item.title: Melinda French Gates: Women, We Deserve Better Than This
item.description: Too many women walk out of their doctors’ office with no diagnosis, no treatment and no plan.
item.dc:creator: Melinda French Gates

item.title: Two Comedians Walk Into a … Friendship
item.description: I just realized why I liked ‘Hacks’ so much.
item.dc:creator: David French
item.category: Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; source image via HBO Max

item.title: Look Closely
item.description: Seeing protests through a camera lens.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: How Black Churches Are Raising Dementia Awareness
item.description: Faith and science are coming together to reduce stigma and improve care in the African American community.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith
item.media:credit: Dustin Chambers for The New York Times
